category:
Category: TAX

Explanation: This appears to be a reconciliation bill that heavily focuses on tax provisions and modifications to the Internal Revenue Code, as evidenced by Title VII which contains multiple tax-related sections including changes to tax rates, deductions, and credits.

extracted_info.title: An Act to provide for reconciliation pursuant to title II of H. Con. Res. 14
extracted_info.scope: Tax code modifications and regulations, particularly focused on individual, corporate, and international tax provisions
key_requirements: Construction of border infrastructure and wall system
Establishment of new tax credits and incentives
Implementation of revised tax rates and deductions
Enhanced reporting requirements for certain entities

metrics.description: Maximum allowable deduction for qualified tips

key_requirement_details.compliance_action: File tax returns with qualified tip deductions

metrics.description: Maximum deduction for qualified overtime compensation

key_requirement_details.compliance_action: Report qualified overtime compensation on tax returns

reporting_requirements.description: Information reporting for Qualified Opportunity Funds
Returns with respect to qualified opportunity funds and qualified rural opportunity funds

monetary_thresholds.description: Minimum student adjusted endowment for applicable educational institutions
Income threshold for limitation on tip deduction

quantitative_limits.description: Floor on charitable contribution deductions for individuals

phased_milestones: Most provisions effective for tax years beginning after December 31, 2024
Some provisions effective for tax years beginning after December 31, 2025
Certain provisions phase out over multiple years through 2034

notes: This is a comprehensive tax reform legislation affecting multiple sections of the Internal Revenue Code
Contains significant modifications to both domestic and international tax provisions
Includes permanent extensions of various temporary tax provisions
